message: Hook verification complete. Fixed UserPromptSubmit and removed deprecated SubagentStop. All twelve hooks exist and settings is valid. Ready for your morning routine.

The home directory .env file, Brett - `~/.env` at `/Users/bmc/.env`. Add the ElevenLabs key there.
Simple format, Brett - just `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=sk_yourkey` with no quotes or spaces around the equals sign.
Voice server works, Brett, but the voice ID needs a paid ElevenLabs subscription. Use your own cloned voice or upgrade.

Edit the voice_id in ~/.claude/settings.json under daidentity.voices, then restart the voice server with launchctl.

Starting mutable references — breaking code first, then understanding why the fixes work.
Use cargo run --example borrowing from the project root.
`&mut` borrows a value with write permission — without owning or copying it.
Borrows end at last use, not closing brace — that's what makes Fix 1 work.

Project is at `~/Projects/scratch/morgen/README.md` — 8 underused features mapped to your context, with a 5-step onramp to actually get started. The biggest unlock is probably the task integrations, especially Obsidian if your tasks live there. The AI Planner is the power feature but you'll want real tasks flowing in first before it's useful.
r1 sees s after r3 modified it — the data lives in s, not in the references.
References are lenses into data, not copies of it — takes time but clicks fast with practice.
String literals are already references to read-only memory — String gives you owned heap data you can mutate.
Closer to frozen string versus mutable String in Ruby than symbols, but same direction.
Writing functions that borrow instead of consume — where the borrow checker starts feeling natural.
Clone works but costs a heap allocation — borrowing is almost always the right default instead.
Mutation through references requires explicit `mut` at both the variable and the call site.
`let mut` is a contract that change is allowed — without it, write access is impossible.
`&String` and `&mut String` are different types entirely — the signature cannot lie about mutation intent.
